mnemonics.title: 2PC Phases Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: VOTE-DECIDE: Remember as 'Votes Decide Destiny' - first participants VOTE, then coordinator DECIDES the fate (commit/abort)

mnemonics.title: 3PC Phases Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: CAN-PRE-DO: Like 'Can we Pre-Do it?' - Can we commit? → Prepare to commit → Do commit! Three steps.

mnemonics.title: Coordinator Role
mnemonics.content: COORDINATOR = Controller: Initiates, Collects Votes, Makes Decision, Communicates Outcome. Think: ICCO - Initiate, Collect, Choose, Output

acronyms.usage: Distributed transaction protocol with Vote and Decision phases
Non-blocking distributed commit protocol with CanCommit, PreCommit, DoCommit phases
Message sent by coordinator to all participants to commit the transaction
Message sent by coordinator to all participants to abort the transaction
Logging technique where logs are written before actual commit to ensure recovery

visualTips.visualization: Draw two columns: Coordinator (top) and Participants (bottom). Arrow from Coordinator to Participants = VOTE-REQUEST. Arrows back = VOTE-COMMIT/VOTE-ABORT. Second set: Coordinator to Participants = GLOBAL-COMMIT/ABORT. Participants to Coordinator = ACK. Show blocking if coordinator fails in middle.

Draw three boxes in sequence: CanCommit → PreCommit → DoCommit. Show decision points (YES/NO arrows). Color code: Green = can commit, Yellow = prepared, Red = abort. Show what happens at each phase if coordinator fails.
Draw participant stuck in middle with question mark. Show coordinator in cloud with X (failed). Draw lock icon on participant resources. Annotate: 'Cannot commit, Cannot abort, Cannot release locks!'

analogies.analogy: Like a jury deliberation - all must agree (vote) for conviction (commit), but judge (coordinator) makes final ruling. If anyone objects (votes abort), case is dismissed.
Like a group restaurant bill split - either everyone pays their share OR no one eats. You can't have some eating and not paying.
Like waiting for your bank to approve a transfer after you've already given cash to the teller - you're stuck until they confirm.
Like having a backup manager who knows what the boss decided, so work can continue even if boss is unavailable.
